---
id: fm-panoptic-segmentation
order: 10
title: Panoptic Segmentation
short_title: Panoptic Segmentation
status: active
summary: Segment detector hits by semantic particle class, particle instance, and interaction grouping.
category: foundation-models
technical_area: computer-vision
physics_area: neutrino event reconstruction
detector_type: LArTPC
technical_method: Computer vision
track: Foundation Model
label: Open Dataset Challenge
datasets:
  - lartpc-fm-v1
metrics:
  - adjusted-rand-index
metric:
  label: Adjusted Rand Index
  goal: Maximize
  documentation: /documentation/metrics.html#adjusted-rand-index
hero_image:
  src: /challenges/fm-panoptic-segmentation.png
  alt: Panoptic segmentation example from the DORAEMON proposal
  position: center center
  caption: Panoptic segmentation example from the DORAEMON proposal.
current_best:
  label: Baseline
  score: "0.412"
facts:
  - label: Category
    value: Physics reconstruction with AI methods
  - label: Detector type
    value: LArTPC
  - label: Technical method
    value: Computer vision
  - label: Timeframe
    value: Active protocol, dates pending
  - label: Dataset type
    value: Sparse LArTPC event records
  - label: Challenge type
    value: Open dataset benchmark
  - label: Technical problem
    value: Computer vision segmentation
links:
  docs: null
  baseline: https://github.com/youngsam/doraemon_site/tree/main/baselines/fm-panoptic-segmentation
  leaderboard: null
bibliography: ../../bibliography.bib
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: mathjax
---

## Physics impact

Panoptic segmentation turns dense detector activity into structured physics objects while preserving particle identity and interaction context for downstream reconstruction. The task borrows the semantic-plus-instance framing used in computer vision [@kirillov2019panoptic] but asks models to respect detector geometry, charge deposition, and sparse event topology.

## Submission shape

A valid entry should be reproducible, versioned, and evaluable on a held-out split without private post-processing. For an event with predicted clusters $\hat{C}$ and reference clusters $C$, the leaderboard will report the adjusted Rand index,

$$
\mathrm{ARI} =
\frac{\sum_{ij}\binom{n_{ij}}{2} -
\left[\sum_i \binom{a_i}{2}\sum_j \binom{b_j}{2}\right] / \binom{n}{2}}
{\frac{1}{2}\left[\sum_i \binom{a_i}{2} + \sum_j \binom{b_j}{2}\right] -
\left[\sum_i \binom{a_i}{2}\sum_j \binom{b_j}{2}\right] / \binom{n}{2}}.
$$

See 

## Leaderboard

Until the challenge opens, the baseline score is the reference value and the leaderboard is treated as provisional. The first public protocol should include a frozen validation container, a baseline model card, and an explicit policy for detector-specific calibration features.
